orchestration.description: Hirobius DesignOps Engine — unit dependency graph for autonomous orchestration. Every unit whose dependsOn entries are all 'done' is eligible for autonomous execution. Do not mark a unit 'done' until its validationCmd exits 0.
stopConditions: A unit's validationCmd fails twice with different error messages — stop and report both errors to human.
A proposed change would modify an output schema already consumed by a 'done' unit — stop and request a schema version bump.
Telemetry shows retry-exhaustion rate > 10% over any 20-generation window — stop and report to human.
Any auth or HMAC check fails in a test — stop immediately, do not attempt to work around.
A unit requires editing more than 3 files that are marked as inputs of other in-progress units — stop and report dependency conflict.

status.done: Code committed, fixtures committed, validationCmd exits 0.
status.pending: Ratified for autonomous execution; all dependsOn done.
status.proposed: Drafted but not yet approved by Adrian. Loop SKIPS proposed.
status.needs-grilling: Spec too fuzzy for autonomous execution; needs spec session.
status.parked: Tracked but explicitly out of the active queue. Loop SKIPS parked.
status.partial: Code exists but validationCmd does not pass cleanly.
status.in-progress: Currently being executed (e.g., in a worktree branch).

definitions.validationCmd: Shell command that MUST exit 0 for the unit to be marked done. Run from repo root.

HDSLayout architectural split: 1918 LoC → 5 modules + slot-based shell
units.description: OPUS-CLASS architectural decision. After all the focused bloat extracts land, refactor HDSLayout from a 1918-LoC do-everything shell to a slot-based composition: HdsShell (skeleton), HdsShellNavRail (sticky sidebar + active-route + nav scroll-hide hook), HdsShellTopBar (theme + language + search + mobile-menu trigger), HdsShellContent (main outlet + right-rail TOC), HdsShellMobiusLayer (3D scene anchor binding). Each child consumes useTheme/useLanguage/useToc directly — no more isDark prop drilling. Boundaries must preserve all existing behavior; visual regression must be byte-identical. This is the kind of split that earns or wastes the next 6 months of velocity.
units.validationCmd: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:visual && pnpm test:layout
agentNotes: DO NOT execute mechanically — this needs an architectural session with Adrian first.
Output: a docs/architecture/HDSLayout-split-plan.md with the 5-module proposal, the dependency graph, the migration sequence, the blast-radius for each public-API change.
Specifically resolve: where does the Mobius nav scroll-hide animation belong? Probably in HdsShellNavRail since it owns the nav.
Resolve: TocProvider wraps children today; should it move into HdsShellContent or stay outside?
Resolve: HDS_NAV_SECTIONS lives in the file today; should it move to src/app/data/nav.ts since 12i-bloat-hdslayout-hds-nav-dedup will already be done?
Smoke test: visual regression must be byte-identical. If it isn't, the split changed semantics — investigate before committing.

units.description: Mobius scene has performance-tier flag in the store but no measured FPS budget per tier. Wire instrumentation: at scene mount, sample frame time over 60 frames and emit to telemetry. In CI (a new tests/mobius-perf.spec.ts) run 120 frames with throttled CPU 4x, assert mean frame time <= 16.6ms (60fps) on desktop tier and <= 33ms (30fps) on mobile tier. Failure blocks PR. This catches the 'Mobius regression nobody noticed' that 12i-bloat-mobius-selector-consolidation might hide.
agentNotes: Add useFrame sampler that pushes frame deltas to a ring buffer.
On 60th frame, emit telemetry event mobius.perf with mean/p95/p99.
Add tests/mobius-perf.spec.ts: navigate to a route with the scene, throttle CPU 4x, run 120 frames, read computed FPS from window.__mobiusFPS or similar exposed test hook.
Add to .github/workflows/quality.yml or a separate perf workflow. Don't let it gate too aggressively first run; collect baseline first.
Document the per-tier targets in src/app/components/shaders/README.md or similar.

units.name: Concrete Creations — AI content repurposing pipeline (Hormozi model)
units.description: Build an AI-driven content repurposing pipeline for Concrete Creations social marketing. Hormozi model: one long-form shoot (product demo / making-of video) → AI extracts mid-length clips + short-form (Reels/Shorts/TikTok) + text posts (threads, carousels, blog). Tools: Descript or CapCut for video, Repurpose.io or n8n for scheduling, Claude for copy reframing. Goal: 20-50 posts/week from a single monthly product shoot. Needs spec session to define: which platforms, posting cadence, brand voice rules, whether to build custom n8n automation or use off-the-shelf scheduler.
agentNotes: Reference: Hormozi AI social media system — long-form → clips → short-form → text, 250+ posts/week from one idea.
Concrete Creations context: handmade concrete products, WA State LLC, visual-first brand. Content = product close-ups, process shots, finished-piece reveals.
Key decision: custom n8n pipeline vs Repurpose.io/Metricool. Custom gives more control over brand voice injection; off-the-shelf is faster to launch.
Must align with HDS token system for any branded graphics output (carousels, quote cards). Use existing asset pipeline from 12u-cc-handmade-asset-pipeline.
T4 strategic — needs Adrian spec session before any code.
units.grillDecision: No active posting yet — platform selection deferred to execution. Off-the-shelf scheduler (Repurpose.io or Metricool) preferred over custom n8n. Decided: 2026-05-03T03:24:55.625Z

units.name: Concrete Creations — Claude+Blender connector for AI product renders
units.description: Evaluate and integrate Claude official Blender connector for AI-assisted product visualization. Concrete products are handmade with natural variance — Blender renders could supplement real photography with consistent hero shots, material/colorway previews, and scene compositions. Claude Blender connector allows Claude to operate inside Blender via MCP: generate scenes, adjust lighting, render product shots via natural language. Evaluate for: (1) consistent product hero renders as catalog grows, (2) colorway/material variant previews without re-shooting, (3) integration with 12u-cc-handmade-asset-pipeline. Needs spec session to decide render vs photo strategy.
agentNotes: Reference video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0kMhtqYBe4Y — Claude official Blender connector demo.
Claude Blender connector = MCP server; Claude drives Blender via natural language (scene setup, lighting, render triggers).
Concrete Creations use case: handmade variance means every piece is unique — renders for catalog consistency vs authenticity tradeoff needs Adrian decision.
Downstream feeds: 12u-cc-ai-content-repurpose-pipeline (renders as social content source), 12u-cc-product-catalog-data-model (render URLs as variant images).
T4 strategic — needs spec session before any code.
units.grillDecision: Renders supplement real photos and cover variants/colorways until photo library is built. Not a replacement strategy. Decided: 2026-05-03T03:24:55.625Z

units.name: Figma masters visually mirror shadcn React render (multi-tenant)
units.description: pipeline/figma-masters-batch.mjs custom trees (buttonStateTree L343-490, inputStateTree, button-like cases in defaultTreeFor) use a hardcoded `C` palette of fixed hex values and a hardcoded `SIZES` constant that don't reflect the live shadcn role-based tokens (role.primary, role.primary-foreground, role.input, role.radius) or the cva variant dimensions in src/app/components/button.tsx (h-8/h-10/h-12, px-3/px-4/px-6, gap-2, rounded-md). Result: Step 4 produces masters with correct slot+variant+componentProperty structure but visually do NOT look like the React component — labels barely visible, colors hardcoded, dimensions wrong. This blocks the multi-tenant-Figma leverage story: designers opening a tenant-themed Figma master must see the actual rendered component (including tenant brand overrides via patchPaletteForTenant). Discovered live during plugin verification 2026-05-04.
units.validationCmd: node pipeline/figma-masters-batch.mjs --tenant=_template && node pipeline/figma-masters-batch.mjs --tenant=concrete-creations && node scripts/test-figma-masters-snapshot.mjs --update
agentNotes: Source of truth for visual spec: src/app/components/button.tsx cva variants (the running React render). Match dimensions, gap, radius, fills against the cva config — not the hardcoded SIZES at L350.

Source of truth for token values: build-tokens.mjs output / hirobius.tokens.json. Resolve role.primary, role.primary-foreground, role.input, role.radius to live hex at build time; do NOT hardcode in the C palette anymore.

Multi-tenant: patchPaletteForTenant(tenantTokens) at L900 already exists. Extend it so role.* aliases patch when tenant overrides exist (currently it only patches the legacy primitive palette). Test with tenant=concrete-creations to confirm CC brand colors propagate to the master.
Same-shape rewrites needed for inputStateTree (similar hardcoded approach) and the Alert / Badge / Tag cases inside defaultTreeFor (also use C.* literals).
Out of scope: components without custom trees (Stack, Grid, Container) — they emit generic baseFrame placeholders; visual fidelity not the goal.
Out of scope: motion/animation states — masters are static; the cinematic-link / cloth-sim / motion-graphics flows are separate tracks.
After rewrite, refresh fixtures/figma-masters/snapshot-pre-8v3.json baseline (closes 12q-figma-snapshot-baseline as a side-effect).
Visual verification: side-by-side Figma master variant export vs React Storybook render. No automated visual-diff gate exists yet — eyeball pass + snapshot lock is the current bar.

Effort estimate: 6-12h. opus + high effort because: (1) cross-cutting architectural call on token-resolution-at-build vs runtime-fetch, (2) ~5 custom-tree functions to rewrite, (3) multi-tenant edge cases, (4) snapshot baseline regen blast radius.

units.validationCmd: node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel manual --emit-inventory /tmp/13y-0-verify.json && node -e "const d=require('/tmp/13y-0-verify.json'); const broken=d.gates.filter(g=>['audit-batch-deliverables','check-unit-overlap','audit-sbom'].includes(g.id)&&g.exitCode!==0); if(broken.length>0){console.error('still failing:',broken.map(b=>b.id));process.exit(1)} console.log('all 3 fixed')"

For audit-sbom: replace @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm with @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-pnpm (community package: cdxgen also works) — uninstall the npm one, install the pnpm one. Update audit-sbom.mjs to invoke the new binary.

useMediaQuery hook may already exist; check src/lib/hooks/ before importing a new one.

units.name: Document or exempt 5 undocumented components
units.description: check-component-docs reports 5 undocumented components (current fidelity D, 24/35 = 69%): agent-tag.tsx, empty-state.tsx, phase-header.tsx, stacked-card-rail.tsx, status-tile.tsx. Either add HDS doc entries (in src/app/pages/hds/components/*.tsx) or add @doc-exempt: <reason> for internal-only components.
agentNotes: agent-tag, phase-header, status-tile likely belong in DisplayPage. empty-state likely in FeedbackPage. stacked-card-rail in DisplayPage.
Mirror the pattern of existing entries — short usage description + a code example. Do not invent new doc layouts.
If a component is truly internal (only used by ops/ surfaces), use @doc-exempt: ops-internal instead of writing doc entries.

units.name: Register 36 unknown exemption markers
units.description: check-exemptions reports 36 unknown markers in code: outline-ok (8), eyebrow-ok (8), token-path-ok (5), link-ok (6), doc-structure-ok (3), binding-ok, replace-ok. These are used in source but not registered in the exemption-marker config. Either register each marker (with rules for what it suppresses) or replace with a registered equivalent.
agentNotes: Find the canonical registration file by reading scripts/check-exemptions.mjs — likely audit-exceptions.config.json or similar.
Each new marker entry needs: name, scope (which gate it suppresses), and a one-line rationale.
The markers have semantic intent: outline-ok suppresses outline-rule findings, eyebrow-ok suppresses eyebrow-typography findings, etc. Match each marker to its suppressed rule.

units.name: Fix 1 broken external link (404)
units.description: check-external-links reports https://github.com/hirobius/design-system/blob/main/docs/guardrails/strength-report.md → 404. The repo or path is wrong (HARD RULE: never push, so the GitHub mirror may not exist). Find the source reference (grep for the URL) and replace with the local docs/guardrails/strength-report.md path or remove if redundant.
agentNotes: Grep for the exact URL across the repo to find the referencing file.
Replace with a local relative link (e.g. ../guardrails/strength-report.md) since the GitHub mirror does not exist per HARD RULE.

units.name: Resolve 4 public-API breaking changes (HdsButton/HdsButtonGroup/HdsSurface/Surface removed)
units.description: check-public-api reports 4 breaking changes: ./app/components/{HdsButton, HdsButtonGroup, HdsSurface, Surface} removed from public exports. Replacements exist (./app/components/{button, callout, field, stat, status-list-item, surface} added). Decision: (a) bump major version + run pnpm api:update to accept, OR (b) restore the old exports as alias re-exports for backwards-compat. Adrian-attended because this is a public-API decision with downstream consumer impact (CC, lilac-insure, ranch-foundation).
agentNotes: Adrian-attended only — do not auto-dispatch.
Recommended path: (b) re-export aliases to avoid breaking the in-flight CC consumer. Add `export { Button as HdsButton } from "./button"` for each removed export.

units.name: Refresh Figma plugin variable export to match repo source
units.description: audit-figma-system reports drift: 305 tokens in source vs 257 in REST payload. Specific deltas: Primitives missing 8 tokens (radius/12, shadow/color, typography/family/display, typography/letterSpacing/caps, typography/lineHeight/{relaxed,snug,none}, typography/size/7xl, typography/weight/light); Component/button has 18 sub-tokens missing (button/size/{lg,md,sm}/*) but extra flat ones. Run the Figma plugin export from the latest source tokens and re-sync.
agentNotes: Adrian-attended — requires the Figma plugin to be open and connected to the design file.
Sequence: (1) run pnpm tokens:export to regenerate hirobius.figma-variables.json, (2) open Figma plugin, (3) trigger export to file, (4) commit hirobius.figma-variables-api.json refresh.

units.name: Delete one-off root files + extend .gitignore
Compact 387 done orchestration units
units.description: Run scripts/compact-orchestration.mjs to compress already-done units. Last invocation (commit 1644294e) yielded -32% size reduction. Done units retain id/name/completedAt/cluster fields; verbose fields (description, agentNotes, validationCmd) compact to nothing since they are no longer load-bearing once a unit is done.
agentNotes: Run scripts/compact-orchestration.mjs (no flags). It writes orchestration.json in place.
Verify validate-orchestration.mjs still passes after compaction.
Verify the watchdog can still parse orchestration.json (run scripts/list-eligible.mjs as a smoke check).

units.name: Delete docs/archive/ entirely (27 stale files)
Move past-session docs/ai status/handoff files into docs/ai/archive/
Move docs/knowledge/grow/youtube/ to gitignored data/knowledge/
Apply 6 gate-cluster merges from gate-audit-2026-05-06.md
units.description: Apply the 6 cluster merges identified in docs/guardrails/gate-audit-2026-05-06.md "Recommended consolidation" section: (1) check-token-paths + check-token-paths-ratchet → ratchet keeps; (2) check-token-descriptions + check-token-description-quality → unified gate with --quality flag; (3) check-hardcoded-fonts + check-font-files + audit-typography-overrides → check-typography-discipline; (4) check-inline-styles + check-style-prop-values + check-css-values → check-style-discipline; (5) audit-components + check-component-completeness + check-component-docs + check-public-api → audit-component-integrity with sub-modes; (6) check-doc-references + check-external-links + check-route-links → check-link-integrity. Net: 17 scripts → 6, ~50 files removed (scripts + fixtures), 85 gates → ~74.
units.validationCmd: node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel pre-commit --emit-inventory /tmp/13z-6-pc.json && node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel pnpm-meta --emit-inventory /tmp/13z-6-pm.json && node scripts/validate-guardrail-registry.mjs && node scripts/validate-fixture-proof-of-firing.mjs
agentNotes: For each merge: (1) write the new merged script that supports the combined check surface (with sub-mode flags where the merged gates differ in scope); (2) update docs/guardrails/registry.json to remove the merged-away entries and update the kept entry with the new responsibilities; (3) git rm the obsoleted scripts and their fixtures/<id>/ directories; (4) update package.json scripts (collapse the obsolete pnpm aliases to point to the new gate or delete them); (5) update any docs/ai/orchestration.json validationCmd that references a deleted script to point to the merged replacement.

After each merge: run pnpm typecheck && node scripts/validate-guardrail-registry.mjs && node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel pre-commit. Commit each merge as a separate commit (chore(guardrails): merge X into Y) so the diff is reviewable.
Use isolation: worktree because this is multi-script multi-fixture multi-doc.

CRITICAL: do NOT delete a gate that has caught real findings in /tmp/gate-audit/*.json — those gates earned their slot. The audit doc lists which 19 pnpm-meta gates failed with real findings; merge but do not delete.

units.name: Audit scripts/ for unreferenced one-offs and prune
units.description: scripts/ has 196 top-level scripts. Many are likely one-off migrations or superseded utilities. Audit each non-(check|audit|run|validate|build|generate|sync|update|verify|compact)-prefixed script for: (a) referenced by package.json scripts? (b) referenced by another script? (c) referenced by docs/ai/orchestration.json validationCmd? (d) referenced in CI workflows or husky hooks? If none of the above: delete. Examples to investigate: figma-console-snippet.js, hds-jsx-compiler.mjs, headless-scan.browser.js, merge-squash.mjs, batch-scan.mjs, normalize-figma-snapshot.mjs.
units.validationCmd: node scripts/validate-orchestration.mjs && pnpm typecheck && node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel pre-commit
agentNotes: For each candidate script, run: git grep "<script-name>" -- package.json scripts/ docs/ .husky/ .github/ — if zero hits outside the script itself, delete is safe.
List every deletion in the commit message with the verification command output as evidence.
Skip scripts in scripts/lib/ (these are shared utilities; treat as load-bearing unless explicit grep proves otherwise).
Sequenced after 13z-6 because the gate merge may delete additional scripts; auditing first then deleting after gate merge would double-count.

hermes_kanban.title: HDSLayout architectural split: 1918 LoC → 5 modules + slot-based shell
hermes_kanban.body:
# HDSLayout architectural split: 1918 LoC → 5 modules + slot-based shell

OPUS-CLASS architectural decision. After all the focused bloat extracts land, refactor HDSLayout from a 1918-LoC do-everything shell to a slot-based composition: HdsShell (skeleton), HdsShellNavRail (sticky sidebar + active-route + nav scroll-hide hook), HdsShellTopBar (theme + language + search + mobile-menu trigger), HdsShellContent (main outlet + right-rail TOC), HdsShellMobiusLayer (3D scene anchor binding). Each child consumes useTheme/useLanguage/useToc directly — no more isDark prop drilling. Boundaries must preserve all existing behavior; visual regression must be byte-identical. This is the kind of split that earns or wastes the next 6 months of velocity.

## Agent notes
- DO NOT execute mechanically — this needs an architectural session with Adrian first.
- Output: a docs/architecture/HDSLayout-split-plan.md with the 5-module proposal, the dependency graph, the migration sequence, the blast-radius for each public-API change.
- Specifically resolve: where does the Mobius nav scroll-hide animation belong? Probably in HdsShellNavRail since it owns the nav.
- Resolve: TocProvider wraps children today; should it move into HdsShellContent or stay outside?
- Resolve: HDS_NAV_SECTIONS lives in the file today; should it move to src/app/data/nav.ts since 12i-bloat-hdslayout-hds-nav-dedup will already be done?
- Smoke test: visual regression must be byte-identical. If it isn't, the split changed semantics — investigate before committing.
- DEFAULT (Adrian 2026-05-02): opus-class slot-based composition refactor. test:visual catches regressions per phase. T4 + ledger.

## Validation command
```
pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:visual && pnpm test:layout
```

- 12i-bloat-hdslayout-health-rail-extract

- 12i-bloat-isdark-prop-drilling

hermes_kanban.title: Mobius perf budget: per-device-tier FPS floors + measured profile in CI
hermes_kanban.body:
# Mobius perf budget: per-device-tier FPS floors + measured profile in CI

Mobius scene has performance-tier flag in the store but no measured FPS budget per tier. Wire instrumentation: at scene mount, sample frame time over 60 frames and emit to telemetry. In CI (a new tests/mobius-perf.spec.ts) run 120 frames with throttled CPU 4x, assert mean frame time <= 16.6ms (60fps) on desktop tier and <= 33ms (30fps) on mobile tier. Failure blocks PR. This catches the 'Mobius regression nobody noticed' that 12i-bloat-mobius-selector-consolidation might hide.

## Agent notes
- Add useFrame sampler that pushes frame deltas to a ring buffer.
- On 60th frame, emit telemetry event mobius.perf with mean/p95/p99.
- Add tests/mobius-perf.spec.ts: navigate to a route with the scene, throttle CPU 4x, run 120 frames, read computed FPS from window.__mobiusFPS or similar exposed test hook.
- Add to .github/workflows/quality.yml or a separate perf workflow. Don't let it gate too aggressively first run; collect baseline first.
- Document the per-tier targets in src/app/components/shaders/README.md or similar.
- DEFAULT (Adrian 2026-05-02): wire telemetry sample-on-mount, fail CI on degradation. T3 + ledger.

## Validation command
```
pnpm exec playwright test tests/mobius-perf.spec.ts
```

hermes_kanban.title: Three.js scene budget per device tier — GPU mem + draw calls + vertices
hermes_kanban.body:
# Three.js scene budget per device tier — GPU mem + draw calls + vertices

mobiusStore has a performance-tier flag but no measured budget per tier. Define: desktop (high tier) — 60fps, ≤4ms frame, ≤80MB GPU; tablet (medium) — 30fps, ≤8ms, ≤40MB; mobile (low) — 30fps with degraded quality, ≤16ms, ≤20MB. Wire renderer.info.memory readings into telemetry. Fail build if a render config would exceed.

## Agent notes
- Read three.js renderer.info during useFrame. Sample memory.geometries, memory.textures, render.calls, render.triangles.
- Per-tier thresholds documented in src/app/stores/mobiusStore.ts.
- Test reads thresholds and fails on exceed.
- If we exceed mobile-tier budget, the fix is degradation (fewer segments, smaller textures), not raising the budget.
- DEFAULT (Adrian 2026-05-02): use budgets named in description — desktop 60fps/4ms/80MB, tablet 30fps/8ms/40MB, mobile 30fps degraded. T2.

## Validation command
```
pnpm exec playwright test tests/mobius-perf.spec.ts
```

Applies [data-tenant='concrete-creations'] scope per the multi-tenant decision. Uses Stripe Checkout (hosted) for payments.

## Agent notes
- Decide: separate repo or workspace package within this monorepo? Workspace is faster but couples deploys.

- Set up Vercel project with custom domain (concrete-creations.<tld>).

- PARKED until multi-tenant architecture decision lands.
- Likely: modes are parallel CSS layers (@layer modes.high-contrast).
- high-contrast prefers-contrast: more media query.
- Sepia is custom; user toggle, not OS.
- Reduced-motion already has a separate validator path.
- [2026-05-03 triage]: validationCmd is sentinel (echo parked). Architectural decision needed before execution: how high-contrast/sepia/reduced-motion modes compose with multi-tenant token overrides. Requires design+spec session.

# Snapshot staleness detector — warn when baseline is older than its source

9d-10's snapshot regression gate locks current state. When source content changes (Pod 1 elevation, Pod 6 typography) but baseline isn't rebaked, gate fails next PR with stale-baseline-vs-correct-content. Add warn-only check: for each baseline, compare last-edit timestamp to latest commit on its source page; if baseline is N+ commits behind, post PR comment.

## Agent notes
- scripts/check-snapshot-staleness.mjs: walk tests/*.spec.ts-snapshots/* files, find source page generators, compare git log dates.
- If baseline is N commits behind source: warn (don't fail).
- Post PR comment via gh CLI.
- Separate workflow alongside visual.yml; non-blocking.

hermes_kanban.body:
# Agency dashboard — codeburn cost observability + moving parts spec

Two-part unit: (1) Evaluate github.com/getagentseal/codeburn as the AI-cost-observability layer for a personal agency dashboard. codeburn reads local JSONL/SQLite session files from 16 AI providers offline (no API keys), classifies tasks into 13 types, detects waste (reread files, low efficiency), tracks yield (AI sessions correlated with git commits), outputs daily/project/model cost breakdowns. (2) Spec the broader personal agency dashboard — a single pane of glass for the solo-founder agency covering: AI cost (codeburn), build velocity (orchestration burn-down), client pipeline, Concrete Creations e-commerce metrics, token spend across Kimi + Claude. Needs-grilling: scope and moving parts need a spec session with Adrian before autonomous execution.

## Agent notes
- codeburn: https://github.com/getagentseal/codeburn — TypeScript TUI + Swift menubar, parses session files, no API keys needed. Potentially drop-in for our MOONSHOT + Anthropic session files.
- Agency dashboard moving parts (known so far): AI cost observability, build velocity / orchestration burndown, client pipeline CRM, Concrete Creations Stripe + Vercel metrics, multi-tenant HDS token overrides.
- This is a T4 strategic unit — needs Adrian spec session before any code. Do not execute autonomously.
- Low priority. Park until Adrian explicitly revives with a fleshed-out spec.

hermes_kanban.title: Concrete Creations — AI content repurposing pipeline (Hormozi model)
hermes_kanban.body:
# Concrete Creations — AI content repurposing pipeline (Hormozi model)

Build an AI-driven content repurposing pipeline for Concrete Creations social marketing. Hormozi model: one long-form shoot (product demo / making-of video) → AI extracts mid-length clips + short-form (Reels/Shorts/TikTok) + text posts (threads, carousels, blog). Tools: Descript or CapCut for video, Repurpose.io or n8n for scheduling, Claude for copy reframing. Goal: 20-50 posts/week from a single monthly product shoot. Needs spec session to define: which platforms, posting cadence, brand voice rules, whether to build custom n8n automation or use off-the-shelf scheduler.

## Agent notes
- Reference: Hormozi AI social media system — long-form → clips → short-form → text, 250+ posts/week from one idea.
- Concrete Creations context: handmade concrete products, WA State LLC, visual-first brand. Content = product close-ups, process shots, finished-piece reveals.
- Key decision: custom n8n pipeline vs Repurpose.io/Metricool. Custom gives more control over brand voice injection; off-the-shelf is faster to launch.
- Must align with HDS token system for any branded graphics output (carousels, quote cards). Use existing asset pipeline from 12u-cc-handmade-asset-pipeline.
- T4 strategic — needs Adrian spec session before any code.

hermes_kanban.body:
# Concrete Creations — Claude+Blender connector for AI product renders

Evaluate and integrate Claude official Blender connector for AI-assisted product visualization. Concrete products are handmade with natural variance — Blender renders could supplement real photography with consistent hero shots, material/colorway previews, and scene compositions. Claude Blender connector allows Claude to operate inside Blender via MCP: generate scenes, adjust lighting, render product shots via natural language. Evaluate for: (1) consistent product hero renders as catalog grows, (2) colorway/material variant previews without re-shooting, (3) integration with 12u-cc-handmade-asset-pipeline. Needs spec session to decide render vs photo strategy.

## Agent notes
- Reference video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0kMhtqYBe4Y — Claude official Blender connector demo.
- Claude Blender connector = MCP server; Claude drives Blender via natural language (scene setup, lighting, render triggers).
- Concrete Creations use case: handmade variance means every piece is unique — renders for catalog consistency vs authenticity tradeoff needs Adrian decision.
- Downstream feeds: 12u-cc-ai-content-repurpose-pipeline (renders as social content source), 12u-cc-product-catalog-data-model (render URLs as variant images).
- T4 strategic — needs spec session before any code.

hermes_kanban.title: Tokenize Concrete Creations brand colors in src/styles/tenants.css
hermes_kanban.body:
# Tokenize Concrete Creations brand colors in src/styles/tenants.css

src/styles/tenants.css contains 5+ raw hex values for the Concrete Creations brand (#BC9D7E, #D0B492, #3A2E1F, etc). The multi-tenant token override pipeline expects these to flow through hirobius.tokens.json → generated CSS vars, not as raw hex. Add tenant-scoped semantic tokens in hirobius.tokens.json for cc, regen tokens, replace hex in tenants.css with var(--semantic-color-...) refs.

## Agent notes
- Source of audit: docs/ai/scan-logs/2026-05-03/check_css-values.log
- Affected file: src/styles/tenants.css lines 24-28+
- Pattern: extend tenant token block in hirobius.tokens.json under $extensions["com.figma.variables"].modes per existing concrete-creations tenant scaffolding
- Run pnpm sync:health (which build-tokens) after edits to regen tenants.css
- [claude-opus 2026-05-06T13:53:33.190Z]: tenant overlay hex values are primitive palette by design (ADR-0001) — they cannot themselves be vars, that is where the chain ends. Fixed at the build script: build-tokens.mjs now appends /* css-ok: tenant palette primitive */ to each generated tenant var line. Regenerated tenants.css. check:css-values passes.

## Validation command
```
pnpm check:css-values
```

hermes_kanban.title: Figma masters visually mirror shadcn React render (multi-tenant)
hermes_kanban.body:
# Figma masters visually mirror shadcn React render (multi-tenant)

pipeline/figma-masters-batch.mjs custom trees (buttonStateTree L343-490, inputStateTree, button-like cases in defaultTreeFor) use a hardcoded `C` palette of fixed hex values and a hardcoded `SIZES` constant that don't reflect the live shadcn role-based tokens (role.primary, role.primary-foreground, role.input, role.radius) or the cva variant dimensions in src/app/components/button.tsx (h-8/h-10/h-12, px-3/px-4/px-6, gap-2, rounded-md). Result: Step 4 produces masters with correct slot+variant+componentProperty structure but visually do NOT look like the React component — labels barely visible, colors hardcoded, dimensions wrong. This blocks the multi-tenant-Figma leverage story: designers opening a tenant-themed Figma master must see the actual rendered component (including tenant brand overrides via patchPaletteForTenant). Discovered live during plugin verification 2026-05-04.

## Agent notes
- Source of truth for visual spec: src/app/components/button.tsx cva variants (the running React render). Match dimensions, gap, radius, fills against the cva config — not the hardcoded SIZES at L350.
- Source of truth for token values: build-tokens.mjs output / hirobius.tokens.json. Resolve role.primary, role.primary-foreground, role.input, role.radius to live hex at build time; do NOT hardcode in the C palette anymore.
- Multi-tenant: patchPaletteForTenant(tenantTokens) at L900 already exists. Extend it so role.* aliases patch when tenant overrides exist (currently it only patches the legacy primitive palette). Test with tenant=concrete-creations to confirm CC brand colors propagate to the master.
- Same-shape rewrites needed for inputStateTree (similar hardcoded approach) and the Alert / Badge / Tag cases inside defaultTreeFor (also use C.* literals).
- Out of scope: components without custom trees (Stack, Grid, Container) — they emit generic baseFrame placeholders; visual fidelity not the goal.
- Out of scope: motion/animation states — masters are static; the cinematic-link / cloth-sim / motion-graphics flows are separate tracks.
- After rewrite, refresh fixtures/figma-masters/snapshot-pre-8v3.json baseline (closes 12q-figma-snapshot-baseline as a side-effect).
- Visual verification: side-by-side Figma master variant export vs React Storybook render. No automated visual-diff gate exists yet — eyeball pass + snapshot lock is the current bar.
- Effort estimate: 6-12h. opus + high effort because: (1) cross-cutting architectural call on token-resolution-at-build vs runtime-fetch, (2) ~5 custom-tree functions to rewrite, (3) multi-tenant edge cases, (4) snapshot baseline regen blast radius.

## Validation command
```
node pipeline/figma-masters-batch.mjs --tenant=_template && node pipeline/figma-masters-batch.mjs --tenant=concrete-creations && node scripts/test-figma-masters-snapshot.mjs --update
```

hermes_kanban.body:
# SIGNAL.md FIX-NNN ↔ orchestration unit cross-walk

Cross-reference 31 FIX-NNN entries in docs/signal/SIGNAL.md against the 22 12q-audit-burndown-2026-05-03 cluster units (and any other orchestration units). For each FIX entry that is now covered by an orchestration unit: mark in SIGNAL.md as "→ covered by <unit-id>" (append-only — do NOT delete; preserve original entry, add annotation). For each FIX entry NOT covered: leave open; consider promoting to a new orchestration unit if high-leverage. Output should make the relationship between SIGNAL.md FIX registry and live orchestration queue obvious to future agents.

## Agent notes
- Source: docs/signal/SIGNAL.md sections "Future-fix registry (FIX-NNN)" — 31 entries (FIX-001 through FIX-031).
- 12q-audit-burndown-2026-05-03 cluster (22 units) covers many FIX entries directly. Spot-check overlaps: 12q-eslint-burndown-post-import-x adjacent to import-x followup; 12q-exemption-marker-registry covers the 123 HDS exemption markers tech-debt baseline; 12q-prompt-regression-baseline maps to FIX-013 (Reflexion); 12q-knip-dead-code-prune relates to FIX-004 (tech-debt ratio).
- Existing FIX walkbacks already in SIGNAL.md ("~~FIX-009~~ — Demote — covered by orchestration unit ...") show the desired annotation pattern.
- Cross-reference also against docs/architecture/strengths-and-differentiators.md "Things we DON't do that peers DO" table which already maps peer patterns to specific orchestration unit IDs — many FIX entries echo those patterns.
- T2 sonnet — needs reading both FIX entries + unit descriptions; judgment on overlap; SIGNAL.md edit only (no code).
- Validation: at least 10 "→ covered by" annotations in SIGNAL.md (rough heuristic; FIX entries left uncovered should be the truly novel ones).

hermes_kanban.title: Live pod stdout tail on /ops/sessions (Mario observability fix)
hermes_kanban.body:
# Live pod stdout tail on /ops/sessions (Mario observability fix)

Mario Zechner's argument: black-box agents prevent debugging. Polled stdout tail (2s interval) on /ops/sessions for currently-running pods. No websocket/SSE — read tail of telemetry/events.jsonl on a server endpoint that returns last N lines for a given pod id. Render inline under the SessionEvent in the feed.

## Agent notes
- Backend endpoint: GET /api/pod-tail?id=<podId> returns last 50 events for that pod.
- Frontend: useEffect with setInterval 2000ms while pod is in-flight.
- Stop polling once pod terminates (event status=success|failed|aborted).
- [hermes abort 2026-05-06]: validationCmd failed: `pnpm typecheck && grep -q 'pod-tail' src/app/pages/ops/SessionsSection.tsx` — validation failed (exit 1): > @hirobius/design-system@0.3.0 typecheck /home/adrian/projects/adrian-milsap | > tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.typecheck.json
- [hermes abort 2026-05-06]: validationCmd failed: `pnpm typecheck && grep -q 'pod-tail' src/app/pages/ops/SessionsSection.tsx` — validation failed (exit 1): > @hirobius/design-system@0.3.0 typecheck /home/adrian/projects/adrian-milsap | > tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.typecheck.json
- [hermes abort 2026-05-06]: validationCmd failed: `pnpm typecheck && grep -q 'pod-tail' src/app/pages/ops/SessionsSection.tsx` — validation failed (exit 1): > @hirobius/design-system@0.3.0 typecheck /home/adrian/projects/adrian-milsap | > tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.typecheck.json
- [hermes abort 2026-05-06]: validationCmd failed: `pnpm typecheck && grep -q 'pod-tail' src/app/pages/ops/SessionsSection.tsx` — validation failed (exit 1): > @hirobius/design-system@0.3.0 typecheck /home/adrian/projects/adrian-milsap | > tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.typecheck.json
- [claude-opus 2026-05-06T16:19:06.145Z]: 4-file ship — vite middleware GET /api/pod-tail?id=X (reads telemetry/events.jsonl, filters by data.unitId, returns last 50, dev-only); usePodTail(podId, isLive) hook polls every 2s and stops on terminal event; PodTail component renders compact stdout-style line list with loading/unavailable/terminal states; SessionsSection attaches PodTail meta to in-flight (info/neutral status) events when not in compact mode. Mario Zechners argument: black-box agents prevent debugging — polled fetch (no SSE/websocket) keeps it human-debuggable and degrades gracefully when middleware is absent. Verified: pnpm typecheck ✓, grep -q pod-tail SessionsSection.tsx ✓, pnpm test:layout 44/44 ✓.

hermes_kanban.title: Fix the 3 broken gate scripts blocking mark_done
hermes_kanban.body:
# Fix the 3 broken gate scripts blocking mark_done

Three gate scripts crash when run via run-gates.mjs --channel manual: (1) audit-batch-deliverables.mjs throws when called without --units/--since/--batch (every channel-run hits this); (2) check-unit-overlap.mjs requires --unit which run-gates does not pass; (3) audit-sbom.mjs (new) calls cyclonedx-npm which invokes npm ls internally and breaks in pnpm workspaces. audit-batch-deliverables is the agent mark_done blocker per CLAUDE.md §1a.

## Agent notes
- For audit-batch-deliverables: when invoked with no args (channel run), default to scanning the current branch HEAD~5..HEAD or print a friendly OK and exit 0.
- For check-unit-overlap: when invoked with no args, run --self-test and exit 0; the channel run is a smoke check, not a per-unit overlap check.
- For audit-sbom: replace @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm with @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-pnpm (community package: cdxgen also works) — uninstall the npm one, install the pnpm one. Update audit-sbom.mjs to invoke the new binary.
- Do NOT modify the existing gate-output.mjs contract. Each script must still emit valid JSON in --json mode.
- [hermes abort 2026-05-06]: validationCmd failed: `node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel manual --emit-inventory /tmp/13y-0-verify.json && node -e "const d=require('/tmp/13y-0-verify.json'); const broken=d.gates.filter(g=>['audit-batch-deliverables','check-unit-overlap','audit-sbom'].includes(g.id)&&g.exitCode!==0); if(broken.length>0){console.error('still failing:',broken.map(b=>b.id));process.exit(1)} console.log('all 3 fixed')"` — validation failed (exit 1): { |   "violations": [ |     { |       "file": "*", |       "line": null, |       "rule": "SBOM_GENERATION_FAILED", |       "severity": "error", |     } |   ], |   "summary": {}, |   "ok": false | }
- [hermes abort 2026-05-06]: validationCmd failed: `node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel manual --emit-inventory /tmp/13y-0-verify.json && node -e "const d=require('/tmp/13y-0-verify.json'); const broken=d.gates.filter(g=>['audit-batch-deliverables','check-unit-overlap','audit-sbom'].includes(g.id)&&g.exitCode!==0); if(broken.length>0){console.error('still failing:',broken.map(b=>b.id));process.exit(1)} console.log('all 3 fixed')"` — validation failed (exit 1): { |   "violations": [ |     { |       "file": "*", |       "line": null, |       "rule": "SBOM_GENERATION_FAILED", |       "severity": "error", |     } |   ], |   "summary": {}, |   "ok": false | }

## Validation command
```
node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel manual --emit-inventory /tmp/13y-0-verify.json && node -e "const d=require('/tmp/13y-0-verify.json'); const broken=d.gates.filter(g=>['audit-batch-deliverables','check-unit-overlap','audit-sbom'].includes(g.id)&&g.exitCode!==0); if(broken.length>0){console.error('still failing:',broken.map(b=>b.id));process.exit(1)} console.log('all 3 fixed')"
```

hermes_kanban.title: Tokenize src/styles/tenants.css for CC multi-tenant override (CC blocker)
hermes_kanban.body:
# Tokenize src/styles/tenants.css for CC multi-tenant override (CC blocker)

src/styles/tenants.css contains 10+ raw hex codes for the Concrete Creations brand: #8B6F47, #F5EFE7, #A8896A, #BC9D7E, #D0B492, #3A2E1F. These break the multi-tenant theme-override architecture. Replace each with var(--semantic-color-*) or var(--component-tenant-*). For values that ARE the tenant brand (not a semantic mapping), define them as :root CSS custom properties named --component-tenant-cc-* and reference those.

## Agent notes
- Edit ONLY src/styles/tenants.css. Do not touch tokens.css or hirobius.tokens.json.
- Map each hex to the closest existing semantic var first; only fall back to a new --component-tenant-cc-* var if no semantic match exists.
- Run node scripts/check-css-values.mjs after each batch of replacements to track progress.

## Validation command
```
node scripts/check-css-values.mjs && node scripts/check-tenant-tokens.mjs
```

hermes_kanban.title: Apply 6 gate-cluster merges from gate-audit-2026-05-06.md
hermes_kanban.body:
# Apply 6 gate-cluster merges from gate-audit-2026-05-06.md

Apply the 6 cluster merges identified in docs/guardrails/gate-audit-2026-05-06.md "Recommended consolidation" section: (1) check-token-paths + check-token-paths-ratchet → ratchet keeps; (2) check-token-descriptions + check-token-description-quality → unified gate with --quality flag; (3) check-hardcoded-fonts + check-font-files + audit-typography-overrides → check-typography-discipline; (4) check-inline-styles + check-style-prop-values + check-css-values → check-style-discipline; (5) audit-components + check-component-completeness + check-component-docs + check-public-api → audit-component-integrity with sub-modes; (6) check-doc-references + check-external-links + check-route-links → check-link-integrity. Net: 17 scripts → 6, ~50 files removed (scripts + fixtures), 85 gates → ~74.

## Agent notes
- For each merge: (1) write the new merged script that supports the combined check surface (with sub-mode flags where the merged gates differ in scope); (2) update docs/guardrails/registry.json to remove the merged-away entries and update the kept entry with the new responsibilities; (3) git rm the obsoleted scripts and their fixtures/<id>/ directories; (4) update package.json scripts (collapse the obsolete pnpm aliases to point to the new gate or delete them); (5) update any docs/ai/orchestration.json validationCmd that references a deleted script to point to the merged replacement.
- After each merge: run pnpm typecheck && node scripts/validate-guardrail-registry.mjs && node scripts/run-gates.mjs --channel pre-commit. Commit each merge as a separate commit (chore(guardrails): merge X into Y) so the diff is reviewable.
- Use isolation: worktree because this is multi-script multi-fixture multi-doc.
- CRITICAL: do NOT delete a gate that has caught real findings in /tmp/gate-audit/*.json — those gates earned their slot. The audit doc lists which 19 pnpm-meta gates failed with real findings; merge but do not delete.

Reverting to approved for human/stronger-model rerun.

hermes_kanban.title: Audit scripts/ for unreferenced one-offs and prune
hermes_kanban.body:
# Audit scripts/ for unreferenced one-offs and prune

scripts/ has 196 top-level scripts. Many are likely one-off migrations or superseded utilities. Audit each non-(check|audit|run|validate|build|generate|sync|update|verify|compact)-prefixed script for: (a) referenced by package.json scripts? (b) referenced by another script? (c) referenced by docs/ai/orchestration.json validationCmd? (d) referenced in CI workflows or husky hooks? If none of the above: delete. Examples to investigate: figma-console-snippet.js, hds-jsx-compiler.mjs, headless-scan.browser.js, merge-squash.mjs, batch-scan.mjs, normalize-figma-snapshot.mjs.

## Agent notes
- For each candidate script, run: git grep "<script-name>" -- package.json scripts/ docs/ .husky/ .github/ — if zero hits outside the script itself, delete is safe.
- List every deletion in the commit message with the verification command output as evidence.
- Skip scripts in scripts/lib/ (these are shared utilities; treat as load-bearing unless explicit grep proves otherwise).
- Sequenced after 13z-6 because the gate merge may delete additional scripts; auditing first then deleting after gate merge would double-count.

hermes_kanban.title: Clean KIMI_API_KEY from 6 Hermes profile .env files
hermes_kanban.body:
**Why this exists:** Each Hermes profile loads its own ~/.hermes/profiles/<name>/.env independently of the root ~/.hermes/.env. We cleaned the root file 2026-05-06, but the 6 profile copies still contain KIMI_API_KEY, KIMI_BASE_URL, KIMI_CN_API_KEY. Even though the model defaults are now hermes3:latest, the provider auto-detector sees KIMI_API_KEY in the loaded env and picks `kimi-coding` as the provider — so hermes3 calls get routed to the (suspended) Moonshot endpoint and crash with HTTP 429 within ~10s of every dispatch.

Confirmed in agent.log:
- "using main provider kimi-coding (hermes3:latest)" ← model OK, provider wrong
- "credential pool: marking KIMI_API_KEY exhausted (status=429), rotating"
- error: "provider=kimi-coding model=hermes3:latest"

**Hard rule:** CLAUDE.md §0 forbids any agent from reading/writing .env files — only the human can touch these. So this is HITL by definition.

**Files to clean (6):**
- ~/.hermes/profiles/architect/.env
- ~/.hermes/profiles/backend-eng/.env
- ~/.hermes/profiles/docs-writer/.env
- ~/.hermes/profiles/frontend-eng/.env
- ~/.hermes/profiles/reviewer/.env
- ~/.hermes/profiles/validator-author/.env

**Fix command (one-liner — same sed pattern used on the root .env):**
\`\`\`
for f in ~/.hermes/profiles/*/.env; do sed -i.bak.kimi-removal -e '/^# LLM PROVIDER (Kimi/,/^\$/d' -e '/^KIMI_/d' -e '/^# KIMI_/d' -e '/^# Kimi /d' -e '/^# Get your key at: https:\/\/platform\.kimi/d' -e '/^# Keys prefixed sk-kimi-/d' -e '/^# Legacy keys from platform\.moonshot\.ai/d' \"\$f\"; echo \"\$f → \$(grep -ic kimi \"\$f\") kimi lines remaining\"; done
\`\`\`

Each file should report \"2 kimi lines remaining\" — those are the OpenCode product descriptions (factual, not config). Same as the root .env cleanup outcome.

**Verification after:**
1. \`tmux kill-session -t hermes-gateway 2>/dev/null; tmux new-session -d -s hermes-gateway 'hermes gateway run'\`
2. Wait ~75s for first dispatcher tick
3. \`tail -5 ~/.hermes/logs/gateway.log\` should show \`crashed=0\`
4. \`tail -5 ~/.hermes/profiles/frontend-eng/logs/agent.log\` should show provider auto-detect picking local-ollama, not kimi-coding

**State right now (2026-05-06 21:47):** Gateway stopped after one verification cycle showed the same Kimi 429 errors (now with hermes3:latest as model but kimi-coding as provider). Kanban is paused; nothing dispatching until this lands.

Proposed-Unit: dashbd-skill-bundle-self
Urgency: eventually

Two skills for the new Self bucket — Adrian's job-search secondary path needs constantly-fresh portfolio artefacts. (1) Activity log: reads last 7 days of git log across local repos, clusters by scope/theme via Anthropic API, emits markdown bullet list ready for LinkedIn / portfolio 'now' / interview brief. (2) Case-study draft: takes free-form rant or clients/<slug>/notes.md, structures into Context/Problem/Approach/Outcome/Artefacts schema, drops into src/app/data/case-studies/. Both are deterministic shape + LLM for content; ship in same bundle.

Validation: pnpm typecheck

Agent notes:
- Activity log: wraps `git log --since=7.days --pretty` + Anthropic API; no new deps
- Case-study draft: reuses auto-assigner.mjs tier router for sonnet dispatch
- Memory project_job_search_ref.md anchors why this bucket exists

Depends on: dashbd-skillsbar-input-shell

hermes_kanban.title: Sales-bucket skills (CRM minimums for solo agency)
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: dashbd-skill-bundle-sales
Urgency: eventually

Two skills for the new Sales bucket — Adrian runs 3 clients (Lilac, Ranch, Prospect-001 Phil) with no shared 'who's cold' signal. (1) Pipeline view: walks clients/*/status.json + prospect dirs, sorts by lastContactAt, surfaces overdue + recommended next touch (email/call/proposal). (2) Proposal price: pasted scope notes → tiered Starter/Standard/Premium quote (matches business-vision package definitions) → markdown quote block with hours/rate/total/terms. Kills 'what should I charge Phil?' hesitation; enforces margin consistency.

Validation: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:layout

Agent notes:
- Add lastContactAt field to clients/<slug>/status.json shape — small migration
- Pipeline view JSON-out for inline panel; same shape as audit-claims
- Proposal price uses tier definitions from project_business_vision memory

Depends on: dashbd-skillsbar-input-shell

hermes_kanban.title: Client-bucket augmentation: meeting→tasks + weekly client digest
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: dashbd-skill-bundle-client-augment
Urgency: eventually

Two additional Client-bucket skills beyond the doc-extracted suite. (1) Meeting→tasks: reads .txt/.vtt transcript (Granola/Otter export or process-call-recording.mjs output), extracts decisions + action items, appends as proposed units to docs/ai/proposed-units.jsonl tagged with client slug. (2) Client digest: weekly status email draft for chosen client — scans last 7 days of commits touching clients/<slug>/, closed orchestration units; emits 'shipped X, next Y, blocker Z' draft body. Solo agencies live on perceived momentum.

Validation: pnpm typecheck

Agent notes:
- Meeting→tasks reuses proposed-units.jsonl seam — schema already in use
- Client digest: git log --grep=<slug> + clientRegistry.ts; no external deps
- Pairs with existing dashbd-skill-bundle-client-suite (speed-to-lead, follow-up nurture, etc.)

Depends on: dashbd-skillsbar-input-shell

hermes_kanban.title: Quality-gate skills (designer-engineer routine checks)
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: dashbd-skill-bundle-quality-gates
Urgency: eventually

Two industry-tool-first quality buttons (per memory feedback_industry_tool_first.md). (1) A11y sweep: boots local Vite preview, runs @axe-core/cli against pinned URL list (/ops, /, /clients/*), writes violations to docs/audits/a11y-report.json, fails on new criticals. (2) Perf budget: lighthouse CLI against 3-4 anchor routes (/, /ops, client landing), compares to docs/budgets/perf-budget.json, prints delta table, exits non-zero on regressions exceeding budget. Adrian sells a design system — shipping with WCAG violations or perf drift is reputational.

Validation: node scripts/run-a11y-sweep.mjs --json && node scripts/run-perf-budget.mjs --json

Agent notes:
- @axe-core/cli + lighthouse are both single justified deps (per dependency-policy memory)
- Pin URL list in code; not data-driven yet
- Cache scores between runs so regressions are visible (delta vs absolute)

hermes_kanban.title: Drag-drop image + video inputs for visual-ingest / page-clone buttons
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: dashbd-visual-ingest-drag-drop
Urgency: eventually

Adrian screen-records and wants to drop the file directly into the visual-ingest / page-clone tile rather than manage an URL or path. Extend SkillTile input-shell with a 'media' input variant: drag-drop zone accepting image (png/jpg/webp/avif) and video (mp4/webm/mov) files. On drop: POST multipart/form-data to a new /api/skills/upload endpoint that writes the blob to public/assets/_incoming/<timestamp>-<sha8>.<ext>, returns the path; SkillTile fires the parent skill with that path as input. Visual-ingest, page-clone, video-clone all consume.

Validation: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:layout

Agent notes:
- Server endpoint must validate MIME type + file size cap (e.g. 100MB)
- Cleanup policy: _incoming files older than 30 days purged by existing convert-incoming-assets.mjs flow
- Show thumbnail preview in tile after drop (img tag for images, <video> for videos with controls)
- Discriminator on SkillSpec: { input: { kind: 'media', accept: 'image/*,video/*', maxSizeMb: 100 } }

Depends on: dashbd-skillsbar-input-shell

hermes_kanban.title: Auto-research — scheduled research jobs that surface findings to /ops
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: dashbd-auto-research
Urgency: eventually

Companion to the user-triggered research input-shell (dashbd-skill-research-shell): research that runs on a schedule and surfaces findings to a queue without a click. Concept: subscribe to topics (DS trends, competitor moves, indie-hacker patterns, AI papers, hiring-manager keywords); cron job (systemd timer locally, or scheduled action once VPS provisioned per project_vps_deployment memory) runs each subscription daily/weekly; output appended to docs/ai/research-feed.jsonl + surfaced as a /ops 'Research feed' rail with mark-read / save-to-knowledge / ramble-to-backlog actions. Source for patterns + tooling: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uBWuKh1nZ2Y&t=8s — process via youtube-knowledge.mjs (or transcript fetch in a fresh session) before scoping the implementation, since the video almost certainly contains the specific framework Adrian is referencing.

Validation: node scripts/auto-research.mjs --once --json

Agent notes:
- FIRST STEP in a fresh session: ingest the linked YT video via youtube-knowledge.mjs to capture the specific framework Adrian heard
- Subscriptions stored as docs/ai/research-subscriptions.json: [{topic, schedule, source: 'web|repo|youtube|gmail', lastRunAt}]
- Output schema mirrors trace-events: {ts, source, topic, summary, sourceUrl, kind: 'finding|trend|alert'}
- Don't ship cron infra until VPS is up — local systemd-user timer works for now
- Auto-research fires the same dispatch path as user-triggered research; difference is trigger, not pipeline

Depends on: dashbd-skill-research-shell, dashbd-skill-youtube-knowledge

hermes_kanban.title: Adaptive pillar/category field on orchestration units
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: dashbd-task-pillar-classification
Urgency: eventually

High-level themes-of-work taxonomy beyond the existing technical lanes (foundation/ops-center/quality/genui/clients/backlog/misc). Adrian wants to see at-a-glance what business realm his work is touching. Reuse the BUILD/GROW/RUN pillar already established in project_workspace_hq memory + parse-bookmarks classification: BUILD = product/DS/site work, GROW = sales/marketing/content/client acquisition, RUN = ops/infra/hygiene/automation. Add `pillar: 'BUILD' | 'GROW' | 'RUN'` field to orchestration unit schema. Auto-classify existing units via auto-assigner.mjs (gemma4 local) reading description; flag low-confidence for manual review. New units auto-classified at write time. Surface on /ops as a pillar-summary rail (distribution + filter chips) and as a column on the dashbd-task-picker view. 'Adaptive' = LLM-assisted, not hardcoded keyword regex.

Validation: node scripts/classify-pillars.mjs --dry-run --json && node scripts/validate-orchestration-schema.mjs

Agent notes:
- BUILD/GROW/RUN matches existing classification used in parse-bookmarks.mjs and docs/knowledge/{build,grow,run}/ — keep mental model consistent
- Schema migration: add `pillar` to unit type in src/app/pages/ops/agentic-os/data.ts and validate via scripts/validate-manifest.mjs equivalent
- Re-classify trigger: button on /ops + git pre-commit hook for new approved units
- Don't replace `cluster` or `phase`; pillar is orthogonal — it's the WHY (business realm), cluster is the WHAT (technical grouping)
- Could extend later to a 4th 'SELF' pillar (recruiter-facing + founder hygiene from dashbd-skill-bundle-self) if BUILD/GROW/RUN proves too coarse

Depends on: dashbd-task-picker

hermes_kanban.title: Absorb HDS documentation into /ops/atlas
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: atlas-absorb-hds-docs
Urgency: eventually

Bring the HDS doc surface (foundations + components + token explorer + everything under /hds/*) into /ops/atlas as additional tabs or a nested section, so /ops/atlas becomes the single command center for design-system + system-health surfaces. Today HDS docs live at /hds and Atlas only carries components/tokens/pipeline/strength sub-surfaces; this consolidates them. Decisions: (1) embed HDS pages inline as Atlas tabs vs route through to existing /hds pages, (2) what happens to the existing /hds top-level and HDSLayout, (3) whether to retire /hds entirely or keep as a public-facing surface while Atlas is the operator surface.

Validation: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:layout

Agent notes:
- Survey all /hds/* routes in src/app/routes.tsx before designing
- Likely tabs to add to Atlas: Foundations (typography/color/spacing/shape/elevation/motion/breakpoints), Components (already there — extend with full HDS catalog), Tokens (already there — extend with full token explorer)
- Decide separately whether public /hds doc routes stay or get redirected
- Touch DESIGN.md / OPERATING_MAP.md if the architectural relationship between /hds and /ops/atlas changes
hermes_kanban.result: Shipped commit a653de31 on fix/ui-pipeline. Added Foundations tab to /ops/atlas (route-through to /hds/* foundation pages); kept /hds as public-facing surface; documented public/operator distinction in OPERATING_MAP.md. Tabs now: Foundations / Components / Tokens / Pipeline. typecheck=0, test:layout=50/50 pass, pre-commit gates=46/46 pass.

hermes_kanban.title: Working Figma plugin bridge — local bots build layouts with full library + tokens
hermes_kanban.body:
Proposed-Unit: figma-bridge-end-to-end
Urgency: NOW (Adrian 2026-05-09 — wants to start building layouts with local bots)

## Goal
Working Figma plugin bridge end-to-end so a local Ollama bot (hermes3 / qwen2.5-coder) can:
1. **List the full component library** from Figma via the plugin
2. **Build layouts** by placing components from that library
3. **Assign all variables** (color/typography/spacing/shadow/etc.) on placed components
4. **Respect tokens** — every value comes from hirobius.tokens.json, no raw hex/px
5. **Solid execution** — no half-placed nodes, no missing-variant fallbacks, no drift between generated layout and React render

## Why
Adrian wants to start using Figma as a generation surface for layouts the bots produce. Existing infrastructure (scripts/hds-bridge.mjs + figma-agent-plugin/ + scripts/generate-to-figma.mjs) is partially wired but currently has:
- Library variable drift (12q-figma-system-drift — 6 missing primitives, 5 missing semantics, 18 missing button.size.*)
- audit-figma-system gate failing (t_dd835117)
- Plugin/bridge handshake works but the generator hasn't been validated against full component coverage

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `pnpm figma:audit` exits 0 (resolves 12q-figma-system-drift)
- [ ] `node scripts/audit-figma-system.mjs` exits 0 (resolves t_dd835117)
- [ ] `node scripts/figma-parity-check.mjs` reports 100% parity between Figma library and shadcn React render
- [ ] One sample layout: bot generates a moderately complex page (e.g. a hero + cta + 3-card row + footer) via the bridge, every component is from the published library, every variable is assigned, no raw values
- [ ] Sample run is reproducible: `pnpm figma:bridge:smoke` (new script) — kicks bridge, tells local hermes3 to build the sample layout, validates output against expected schema
- [ ] FIGMA_BRIDGE.md (docs/ai/rules/) updated with the working flow
- [ ] Memory pointer updated: project_figma_plugin_priority.md → bridge is shippable, not aspirational

## Pre-reqs (already done)
- 12q-figma-master-shadcn-fidelity (done — masters mirror shadcn render)
- 13y-21-figma-export-refresh (done — variable export refreshed)
- t_6f70e9a0 (done — ROADMAP.md + EXECUTION_PLAN.md saved)

## Validation
```
pnpm figma:audit && node scripts/audit-figma-system.mjs && node scripts/figma-parity-check.mjs && pnpm figma:bridge:smoke
```

## Agent notes
- This is the umbrella task that unblocks Adrian's local-bot-builds-layouts workflow
- Likely needs: regen Figma master from token source (per 12q-figma-system-drift agent notes), fix audit script, write the smoke test, document the flow
- Some sub-tasks are HITL (Adrian must ratify whether Figma vs tokens is canonical) — split out and block this task on those decisions
- Don't half-ship: if the smoke test can't run end-to-end with a live Ollama call, mark blocked instead of completing

Burn down ops/atlas raw transitions, border-radii, tier-bypass, grids
