proposedUnit.name: Investigate B2 OWASP SAMM 88→75 regression
proposedUnit.description: Strength-report B2 dimension dropped from 88 (prev handoff) to 75 (post-regen this session). No agent touched SAMM inputs; either the regen surfaced new data or methodology changed. Read computeB2() in scripts/generate-strength-report.mjs, identify why the score moved, decide whether the new lower score reflects real posture decline or a methodology tightening that should be documented.
proposedUnit.validationCmd: node scripts/generate-strength-report.mjs && node -e "const r=require('./docs/guardrails/strength-report.json'); const b2=r.scoreB.dimensions.find(d=>d.id==='B2'); if(b2.score<75) process.exit(1)"
agentNotes: Compare B2 input data files modified between commits 8b4ae396 and HEAD
If methodology changed, update docs/guardrails/strength-report.md notes
If real regression, propose remediation unit

proposedUnit.name: Investigate B6 OSV/audit 100→95 drift mid-session
proposedUnit.description: B6 came in at 100 on first regen after wiring (commit fe0f17e7) and 95 on later regen. Likely a pnpm audit picked up one new high between regens. Read docs/security/osv-report.json (or rerun pnpm audit --json) to identify which advisory appeared, decide whether action is needed.
agentNotes: Cross-check against fe0f17e7 commit baseline if osv-report.json was committed
If transient/dev-only, document and close
If real, triage upgrade path

proposedUnit.name: check-typography-discipline: skip Windows-drive-letter top-level paths
proposedUnit.description: scripts/check-typography-discipline.mjs walkFonts() uses statSync on every entry and dies with ENOENT when WSL2 lighthouse-ci leaves dirs named literally 'C:\Users\...lighthouse.X' in the worktree. Add a top-level filter that skips entries matching /^[A-Z]:/ (Windows drive letters). One-line patch. Surfaced when B4 Web Vitals dispatch (commit e721a308) leaked 42 such dirs and crashed the pre-commit walker.
proposedUnit.validationCmd: mkdir -p './C:\Users\test\lighthouse.999' && node scripts/check-typography-discipline.mjs; rc=$?; rm -rf './C:\Users\test\lighthouse.999'; exit $rc
agentNotes: The fix is in walkFonts() ~line 83
Test by creating a fake C:\... dir in the worktree, running the gate, confirming it does NOT crash
Don't modify the gate's actual font-checking logic — only the directory walker

proposedUnit.name: Fix audit-figma-system gate failure
proposedUnit.description: audit-figma-system has been failing across recent sessions per multiple handoff docs. Carried forward from handoff-2026-05-06-evening.md. Triage: read script, identify failure mode, fix or mark investigate-broken with reason.
agentNotes: Check whether failure is environmental (missing FIGMA_TOKEN) or logic
If environmental, gate behind env-var presence
If logic, fix; if neither, document in registry.json with investigate-broken status

proposedUnit.name: Triage 7 investigate-broken gates from soft-gates audit
proposedUnit.description: Seven gates flagged investigate-broken in the soft-gates audit (commits 02de1909, 67257a42): check-route-smoke (timeout), check-unit-overlap, check-token-descriptions, check-tier-bypass, check-link-integrity, audit-figma-system (separate unit), audit-pages. Some are real bugs; others may be env-only. Triage one-by-one, fix or document.
agentNotes: Each gate is independent — can be split into 6 sub-units
Some failures are exit-2 (real bugs); check-route-smoke is timeout
Document fixes in commit messages

proposedUnit.name: Tighten kimi drain-notify false-positive surface
proposedUnit.description: Current throttle (commit 14a73474) gates Discord notify on orchestration.json mtime > last-notify timestamp. False-positive: any process touching orchestration.json without shipping work (regen pass, schema migration, manual claim flip with no completion) re-fires the notify. Acceptable for now. Tighten by tracking last-completed-unit timestamp instead of orch mtime, OR by hashing orchestration.json and comparing semantic content.
agentNotes: Don't ship until at least one false-positive is observed in practice
Cheapest fix: hash orchestration.json content (excluding mtime-only fields like lastClaimAt) and compare prev-hash to current
Alternative: track last-completedAt timestamp from orchestration.json units array

proposedUnit.name: Lanes tiles drill into Pipeline tab pre-filtered to lane
proposedUnit.description: All 7 lane tiles on /ops AgenticOSPage land on identical default Atlas Routes tab. LanesGrid.tsx:29 builds /ops/atlas?lane=<id> but AtlasPage.tsx:48-51 reads only window.location.hash, ignoring ?lane=. Fix: link to ...?lane=<id>#pipeline; AtlasPage reads ?lane= and passes initialLane to PipelineDag; PipelineDag accepts initialLane and seeds phaseFilter+clusterFilter from pickLane matches; export unitsForLane helper from lanes.ts.
agentNotes: Original author flagged this in LanesGrid.tsx:6-8 ('best we can do until atlas grows lane-aware deep links')
4 files: LanesGrid.tsx (1 line), AtlasPage.tsx (read query), pipeline-dag.tsx (accept prop, seed filters), lanes.ts (export helper)
Use key={lane ?? 'no-lane'} on PipelineDag to force re-init when navigating tile→tile

proposedUnit.name: Surface youtube-knowledge as Knowledge-bucket button
proposedUnit.description: scripts/youtube-knowledge.mjs is parameterless by default (walks both Google accounts, fetches new likes + Watch Later, summarizes via Hermes, writes to data/knowledge/grow/youtube/, caches processed). Single-line add to skills.ts SKILLS array + skill-runner-middleware.mjs SKILLS map. Caveat: requires .youtube-token-personal.json + .youtube-token-work.json pre-configured per docs/operations/google-oauth-setup.md.
agentNotes: Pattern matches the 8 quick-wins shipped 2026-05-06
No-arg invocation; cache prevents re-fetching processed videos
Set timeoutMs to 600_000 (10min) — transcript fetching is slow; YT API is throttled

proposedUnit.name: SkillTile input-shell variant for input-needing skills
proposedUnit.description: Build SkillTile variant that accepts a single user input before firing — textarea | url | filePicker | clientSlug. Posts to /api/skills/:id with body {input: '<value>'}; skill-runner middleware appends to argv. Unblocks 13 already-built scripts: page-clone, video-clone, visual-ingest, generate-visual-catalog, figma-diff, auto-assigner, promote-learned-rule, sync-client-emails, gpt-knowledge, scaffold-component, scaffold-tenant, process-call-recording, ramble→backlog. Each then ships as a 1-line registration.
agentNotes: Discriminated-union: { input?: { kind: 'text' | 'url' | 'file' | 'slug', label, placeholder } }
Skill-runner middleware needs argv-template support: use ['node', 'scripts/foo.mjs', '${input}'] convention
One reusable component covers all 13 input-needing skills

proposedUnit.name: Wire 13 input-needing buried scripts as buttons via input-shell
proposedUnit.description: Once dashbd-skillsbar-input-shell lands, register all 13 already-built scripts that take args: page-clone, video-clone, visual-ingest, visual-catalog, figma-diff, auto-assigner, promote-learned-rule, sync-client-emails, gpt-knowledge, scaffold-component, scaffold-tenant, process-call-recording, ramble→backlog. Bucket distribution: Build (5), Visual (3), Knowledge (3), Ops (2). 1 SKILLS entry + 1 middleware entry per script.
agentNotes: No new scripts written — only registrations
Input-shape per skill documented in script header
Pre-flight: confirm each script accepts its declared input form (some use --flag, some positional)

proposedUnit.name: 'New skill' capture button + proposed-skills.jsonl seam
proposedUnit.description: Meta-button that lets Adrian describe a skill in natural language; submission appends one line to docs/ai/proposed-skills.jsonl (append-only seam mirroring proposed-units.jsonl). Schema: {ts, name, description, expectedOutput, requestedBy}. The build step happens in the next interactive Claude Code session: Claude reads queue, runs `brainstorming` skill on Adrian to fill gaps, writes the script + skills.ts + middleware entries, commits. Button captures intent only; does NOT trigger code generation.
agentNotes: Build the capture form first; the build loop is human-Claude in-session
File should be gitignored (mirrors proposed-units.jsonl) — local-only append seam
Reject pure-auto build (Claude builds without grilling) — produces low-quality skills that pollute the repo

proposedUnit.name: /lab Staging workspace — Variants/Themes/Research three-tab shell
proposedUnit.description: Single /lab workspace surface unifying three jobs Adrian asked for in 2026-05-06 session: (1) staging area for redesign variants (queue a route → spawn git worktree → preview → approve/reject), (2) Token Theme Explorer (live CSS-custom-property overrides for visual evolution exploration without rebuild), (3) research input-shell (multi-mode: web-search, repo-grep, ingest-url, page-clone, classify-task). One shell, three tabs. Worktree-based variants > file-swap (safer rollback via `git branch -D`).
agentNotes: Surface lives at /lab/staging or /ops with a Staging tab — pick during scoping
Worktree spawn = `git worktree add .worktrees/staging/<variant-id> -b staging/<variant-id>`
Approve = merge worktree branch to fix/ui-pipeline; Reject = `git branch -D staging/<variant-id>`
Theme Explorer reads/writes CSS custom properties at runtime; no rebuild step

proposedUnit.name: Approved-bucket triage command (gap: no dedicated tool today)
proposedUnit.description: Today the approved bucket (26 units across 3 phases as of 2026-05-06) is sifted via Pipeline DAG visual review + manual orchestration.json edits. Gap: no command for re-cluster, archive stale-approved, priority adjust, dependency repair, or 'find approved units no agent has claimed in N days.' Build scripts/triage-approved.mjs (text TUI or --json output) + wire as Ops button. Complements list-eligible.mjs (which just lists), audit-claims.mjs (which finds stale claimed).
agentNotes: Output shape: {byCluster, byAge, withMissingDeps, candidatesForArchive}
Don't auto-modify orchestration.json — emit suggestions; Adrian edits
Ship as JSON+text dual mode like list-eligible.mjs

proposedUnit.name: Save Figma DesignOps planning dumps to docs/figma-plugin/
proposedUnit.description: Adrian pasted two architectural planning docs in 2026-05-06 session that exceed the depth of anything in repo: (1) Gap Analysis + Feature Roadmap (AST Gatekeeper, self-healing retry loop, JSX compiler upgrades, two-way protocol), (2) Phase 0-7 Execution Strategy (orchestration unit graph). Existing docs in repo (adr/004, process/2026-04-19-figma-sync, operations/figma-plugin-runbook) cover only the high-level intent. Save as docs/figma-plugin/ROADMAP.md (the why) + docs/figma-plugin/EXECUTION_PLAN.md (the how) + add memory pointer.
agentNotes: Source: scrollback of session 2026-05-06 (paste blocks under '#Hirobius DesignOps Engine — Architectural Roadmap' and '#Execution Strategy — Autonomous Orchestration Plan')
Two files because they serve different jobs (architectural reasoning vs. unit graph)
Memory pointer in MEMORY.md alongside project_figma_plugin_priority.md

proposedUnit.name: Token-gated /c/:slug client portal
proposedUnit.description: No client-FACING route exists today (/ops/clients/<slug> is internal-only ClientDashboardPage). Build /c/:slug?token=… single-page route: live preview iframe + milestone list + lightweight feedback form. Strategy aligned with memory project_client_portal.md ('show live site only, simple password-gate, lightweight feedback form'). Skip microsites/subdomains — adds infra cost (separate builds, DNS, deployment) not worth the boundary today.
agentNotes: Token = HMAC of slug + secret; rotated per-engagement
Server-side validation OR frontend-only check (acceptable for low-stakes private URLs)
First clients to wire: lilac-insure, the-ranch-foundation

proposedUnit.name: Visual evolution foundation: primitives + vocabulary + theme explorer
proposedUnit.description: Three sequential sub-steps making HDS LLM-promptable for visual direction (per Adrian's 'demo to recruiters' goal): (a) expand spacing primitives px64/px80/px96/px128, add easing curve tokens (easing.standard/enter/exit/spring), add semantic.radius.action — current scale tops at px48 and motion has no easing tokens. (b) Write DESIGN_VOCABULARY.md mapping intent terms to token operations: 'airy' = spacing 1.25-1.5×; 'rounded' = radius.* values; 'minimalist' = reduce border frequency, increase whitespace; 'editorial' = larger type scale, wider line-height. (c) Token Theme Explorer in /lab/staging Themes tab — live CSS-custom-property overrides, no rebuild. (c) doubles as recruiter demo + visual-evolution sandbox.
proposedUnit.validationCmd: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test:layout && node scripts/audit-token-sidecars.mjs
agentNotes: Split into 3 sub-units during triage: visual-evol-a (primitives), visual-evol-b (vocabulary doc), visual-evol-c (explorer)
Adrian's 'circular consumption' system already supports this — gap is range, not architecture
Sub-step (a) ships standalone; (b) needs (a); (c) needs (a)+(b)

proposedUnit.name: AI-powered Build skills bundle (6 sub-skills)
proposedUnit.description: Doc-extracted Build-bucket skills, ship after input-shell exists. Each is a separate sub-unit during triage: (1) Brand touchpoint audit — domain in, pro-bono visual scrub sheet out (Lilac opener move); (2) Tenant theme override — color+font in, instant [data-tenant=…] re-skin (closing-call demo); (3) Layout de-squish — route in, container/grid/text-lockup heuristic out; (4) Promote to core — lab component → src/components/ with 4-check Promotion Checklist; (5) Macro-layout wrap — page → one of 5 macro templates (CaseStudy/Doc/Hub/Lab/SplitFeature); (6) Visual diff heal — local route vs gold, pixelmatch, auto-fix commit if drift > threshold.
agentNotes: Each sub-skill is its own script + skill registration
Ordering: tenant-override + brand-audit first (used in client pitches); promote-to-core + macro-wrap second (DS quality); de-squish + diff-heal third (regression safety)
Source: Google Doc 10xIW4sCMLcE6k9DDSef_sQp3VfD1yTHpmO7xMwK6jM0 brain-dump

proposedUnit.name: Client-bucket skills suite (4 sub-skills)
proposedUnit.description: Doc-extracted Client-bucket skills. Each is a separate sub-unit during triage: (1) Speed-to-lead reply — Gmail watcher → qualifying draft within minutes of inbound lead; (2) DB reactivation — cold contacts → warm-restart drafts in Adrian's voice, queued approve/deny; (3) Follow-up nurture — picked contact → 4-touchpoint sequence (Day 1/3/7/14) personalized to stated pain points; (4) Client pitch bundle — business desc + selected modules → tiered PDF proposal (tight + expanded). All four are services Adrian sells to clients AND personal force-multipliers — running them on himself is demo + time-saver in one.
agentNotes: Pre-req: Gmail OAuth token exists + Anthropic/local LLM access
Speed-to-lead has highest leverage — listed at top of Offerings section in source doc
Source: Google Doc 10xIW4sCMLcE6k9DDSef_sQp3VfD1yTHpmO7xMwK6jM0 'Offerings' section

proposedUnit.name: Token impact trace — blast-radius report per token
proposedUnit.description: Pick a token path in hirobius.tokens.json, return one-screen report: every component/page that consumes it (via build-token-index.mjs map) + automatic Playwright smoke-test of those routes. Turns 'what will this break?' into a 5-second answer. Pitches as Hirobius differentiator — the kind of confidence designers can't get from CSS alone.
agentNotes: Reads token-usage-map.json (output of build-token-index.mjs — already wired as button)
Smoke-test via Playwright on consumer routes; cap at 10 routes for speed
Output: { token, consumers: [{path, line}], routesUsing: [...], smokeResults: [...] }

proposedUnit.name: Daily task picker view — pick from 474 units, queue for today
proposedUnit.description: Tasking UX gap (Adrian 2026-05-06): 'I see 474 units, but when I click a tile I see nothing.' Even after lanes-tile-drilldown fix lands, Pipeline DAG is graph-shaped, not personal-picker shaped. Build /ops 'Tasks' tab: virtualized table filterable by lane × status × tier × cluster × tag; multi-select via checkbox; 'Add to Today' action writes selection to docs/ai/today.json (gitignored); 'Today' subview shows just chosen units with quick-actions (claim, defer, mark blocked, complete). Replaces the 'where do I start' friction.
agentNotes: 474 units is too many for unfiltered render; use react-window or similar virtualization
'Today' state persists to docs/ai/today.json — gitignored, local-only
Quick-actions reuse existing claim/revert mechanics in orchestration.json
Pair filter UI with the Pipeline-tab cluster/phase/status chips for consistency

proposedUnit.name: Self-bucket skills (recruiter-facing + founder hygiene)
proposedUnit.description: Two skills for the new Self bucket — Adrian's job-search secondary path needs constantly-fresh portfolio artefacts. (1) Activity log: reads last 7 days of git log across local repos, clusters by scope/theme via Anthropic API, emits markdown bullet list ready for LinkedIn / portfolio 'now' / interview brief. (2) Case-study draft: takes free-form rant or clients/<slug>/notes.md, structures into Context/Problem/Approach/Outcome/Artefacts schema, drops into src/app/data/case-studies/. Both are deterministic shape + LLM for content; ship in same bundle.
agentNotes: Activity log: wraps `git log --since=7.days --pretty` + Anthropic API; no new deps
Case-study draft: reuses auto-assigner.mjs tier router for sonnet dispatch
Memory project_job_search_ref.md anchors why this bucket exists

proposedUnit.name: Sales-bucket skills (CRM minimums for solo agency)
proposedUnit.description: Two skills for the new Sales bucket — Adrian runs 3 clients (Lilac, Ranch, Prospect-001 Phil) with no shared 'who's cold' signal. (1) Pipeline view: walks clients/*/status.json + prospect dirs, sorts by lastContactAt, surfaces overdue + recommended next touch (email/call/proposal). (2) Proposal price: pasted scope notes → tiered Starter/Standard/Premium quote (matches business-vision package definitions) → markdown quote block with hours/rate/total/terms. Kills 'what should I charge Phil?' hesitation; enforces margin consistency.
agentNotes: Add lastContactAt field to clients/<slug>/status.json shape — small migration
Pipeline view JSON-out for inline panel; same shape as audit-claims
Proposal price uses tier definitions from project_business_vision memory

proposedUnit.name: Client-bucket augmentation: meeting→tasks + weekly client digest
proposedUnit.description: Two additional Client-bucket skills beyond the doc-extracted suite. (1) Meeting→tasks: reads .txt/.vtt transcript (Granola/Otter export or process-call-recording.mjs output), extracts decisions + action items, appends as proposed units to docs/ai/proposed-units.jsonl tagged with client slug. (2) Client digest: weekly status email draft for chosen client — scans last 7 days of commits touching clients/<slug>/, closed orchestration units; emits 'shipped X, next Y, blocker Z' draft body. Solo agencies live on perceived momentum.
agentNotes: Meeting→tasks reuses proposed-units.jsonl seam — schema already in use
Client digest: git log --grep=<slug> + clientRegistry.ts; no external deps
Pairs with existing dashbd-skill-bundle-client-suite (speed-to-lead, follow-up nurture, etc.)

proposedUnit.name: Quality-gate skills (designer-engineer routine checks)
proposedUnit.description: Two industry-tool-first quality buttons (per memory feedback_industry_tool_first.md). (1) A11y sweep: boots local Vite preview, runs @axe-core/cli against pinned URL list (/ops, /, /clients/*), writes violations to docs/audits/a11y-report.json, fails on new criticals. (2) Perf budget: lighthouse CLI against 3-4 anchor routes (/, /ops, client landing), compares to docs/budgets/perf-budget.json, prints delta table, exits non-zero on regressions exceeding budget. Adrian sells a design system — shipping with WCAG violations or perf drift is reputational.
agentNotes: @axe-core/cli + lighthouse are both single justified deps (per dependency-policy memory)
Pin URL list in code; not data-driven yet
Cache scores between runs so regressions are visible (delta vs absolute)

proposedUnit.name: Drag-drop image + video inputs for visual-ingest / page-clone buttons
proposedUnit.description: Adrian screen-records and wants to drop the file directly into the visual-ingest / page-clone tile rather than manage an URL or path. Extend SkillTile input-shell with a 'media' input variant: drag-drop zone accepting image (png/jpg/webp/avif) and video (mp4/webm/mov) files. On drop: POST multipart/form-data to a new /api/skills/upload endpoint that writes the blob to public/assets/_incoming/<timestamp>-<sha8>.<ext>, returns the path; SkillTile fires the parent skill with that path as input. Visual-ingest, page-clone, video-clone all consume.
agentNotes: Server endpoint must validate MIME type + file size cap (e.g. 100MB)
Cleanup policy: _incoming files older than 30 days purged by existing convert-incoming-assets.mjs flow
Show thumbnail preview in tile after drop (img tag for images, <video> for videos with controls)
Discriminator on SkillSpec: { input: { kind: 'media', accept: 'image/*,video/*', maxSizeMb: 100 } }

proposedUnit.name: Auto-research — scheduled research jobs that surface findings to /ops
proposedUnit.description: Companion to the user-triggered research input-shell (dashbd-skill-research-shell): research that runs on a schedule and surfaces findings to a queue without a click. Concept: subscribe to topics (DS trends, competitor moves, indie-hacker patterns, AI papers, hiring-manager keywords); cron job (systemd timer locally, or scheduled action once VPS provisioned per project_vps_deployment memory) runs each subscription daily/weekly; output appended to docs/ai/research-feed.jsonl + surfaced as a /ops 'Research feed' rail with mark-read / save-to-knowledge / ramble-to-backlog actions. Source for patterns + tooling: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uBWuKh1nZ2Y&t=8s — process via youtube-knowledge.mjs (or transcript fetch in a fresh session) before scoping the implementation, since the video almost certainly contains the specific framework Adrian is referencing.
agentNotes: FIRST STEP in a fresh session: ingest the linked YT video via youtube-knowledge.mjs to capture the specific framework Adrian heard
Subscriptions stored as docs/ai/research-subscriptions.json: [{topic, schedule, source: 'web|repo|youtube|gmail', lastRunAt}]
Output schema mirrors trace-events: {ts, source, topic, summary, sourceUrl, kind: 'finding|trend|alert'}
Don't ship cron infra until VPS is up — local systemd-user timer works for now
Auto-research fires the same dispatch path as user-triggered research; difference is trigger, not pipeline

proposedUnit.name: Adaptive pillar/category field on orchestration units
proposedUnit.description: High-level themes-of-work taxonomy beyond the existing technical lanes (foundation/ops-center/quality/genui/clients/backlog/misc). Adrian wants to see at-a-glance what business realm his work is touching. Reuse the BUILD/GROW/RUN pillar already established in project_workspace_hq memory + parse-bookmarks classification: BUILD = product/DS/site work, GROW = sales/marketing/content/client acquisition, RUN = ops/infra/hygiene/automation. Add `pillar: 'BUILD' | 'GROW' | 'RUN'` field to orchestration unit schema. Auto-classify existing units via auto-assigner.mjs (gemma4 local) reading description; flag low-confidence for manual review. New units auto-classified at write time. Surface on /ops as a pillar-summary rail (distribution + filter chips) and as a column on the dashbd-task-picker view. 'Adaptive' = LLM-assisted, not hardcoded keyword regex.
agentNotes: BUILD/GROW/RUN matches existing classification used in parse-bookmarks.mjs and docs/knowledge/{build,grow,run}/ — keep mental model consistent
Schema migration: add `pillar` to unit type in src/app/pages/ops/agentic-os/data.ts and validate via scripts/validate-manifest.mjs equivalent
Re-classify trigger: button on /ops + git pre-commit hook for new approved units
Don't replace `cluster` or `phase`; pillar is orthogonal — it's the WHY (business realm), cluster is the WHAT (technical grouping)
Could extend later to a 4th 'SELF' pillar (recruiter-facing + founder hygiene from dashbd-skill-bundle-self) if BUILD/GROW/RUN proves too coarse

proposedUnit.name: Absorb HDS documentation into /ops/atlas
proposedUnit.description: Bring the HDS doc surface (foundations + components + token explorer + everything under /hds/*) into /ops/atlas as additional tabs or a nested section, so /ops/atlas becomes the single command center for design-system + system-health surfaces. Today HDS docs live at /hds and Atlas only carries components/tokens/pipeline/strength sub-surfaces; this consolidates them. Decisions: (1) embed HDS pages inline as Atlas tabs vs route through to existing /hds pages, (2) what happens to the existing /hds top-level and HDSLayout, (3) whether to retire /hds entirely or keep as a public-facing surface while Atlas is the operator surface.
agentNotes: Survey all /hds/* routes in src/app/routes.tsx before designing

Likely tabs to add to Atlas: Foundations (typography/color/spacing/shape/elevation/motion/breakpoints), Components (already there — extend with full HDS catalog), Tokens (already there — extend with full token explorer)
Decide separately whether public /hds doc routes stay or get redirected
Touch DESIGN.md / OPERATING_MAP.md if the architectural relationship between /hds and /ops/atlas changes
